codeReview.summary: This is a verification-only feature — no production code was authored. The worker ran all required verification steps: 1,791 unit tests (exceeding the 1,768 threshold), clean release build, QEMU visual verification with clock-update pixel diffs proving end-to-end pipeline continuity, integration test (17 boot markers), and 75-second stress test on 4 SMP cores. The grep for DoubleWriter/DoubleReader/copy_front_to_back in services/ returned zero results, confirming production code has fully migrated to TripleWriter/TripleReader. The commit itself (ac981c0) is from the prior feature (damage-tracking-skip-fix) — the integration-verification feature produced no code changes, only verification evidence, which is correct for its role.

issues.description: DoubleWriter, DoubleReader, copy_front_to_back, and DOUBLE_SCENE_SIZE still exist as public types in the scene library (~350 lines of dead production code). While no production service uses them (confirmed by grep), the canonical claim 'When you build a new way, kill the old way — no parallel implementations' suggests they should be deleted. Currently used only in test files (scene.rs, scene_render.rs, unicode.rs). Recommendation: delete from library, update tests to use TripleWriter/TripleReader exclusively.

VAL-CROSS-001 requires typing 20+ characters rapidly and verifying all appear in screenshot. The worker could not automate keyboard input due to a known QEMU limitation (sendkey does not route to virtio-keyboard-device). The worker substituted clock-update pixel diffs (two screenshots 4s apart showing 4,635 changed pixels in the clock region), which proves the Core→Compositor→RenderBackend→GPU pipeline is continuously active. This is a reasonable proxy: the clock text uses the same Glyphs content type, glyph cache, scene graph, and render backend path as typed text. The only untested segment is keyboard input → editor → core write-request dispatch, which is outside the pipeline-transport scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: DoubleWriter/DoubleReader/copy_front_to_back (~350 lines) remain in scene/lib.rs as public dead code after triple-buffer migration. AGENTS.md for this mission notes 'DoubleWriter→TripleWriter' in Key Source Files but doesn't explicitly state that the old types should be deleted. The canonical claim 'When you build a new way, kill the old way' applies but wasn't enforced for library-level dead code. Consider adding a convention: 'Verification features should flag dead code discovered during grep checks for follow-up cleanup.'
sharedStateObservations.evidence: system/libraries/scene/lib.rs lines 1478-1820 contain DoubleWriter, DoubleReader, copy_front_to_back, DOUBLE_SCENE_SIZE, READER_STATE_OFFSET, and supporting functions (back_offset_of, front_of, read_reader_done_gen, write_reader_done_gen). Zero usage in system/services/.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The os-worker SKILL.md still references 'double-buffer protocol' in the 'When to Use This Skill' section ('Scene graph types and double-buffer protocol') and the work procedure ('system/libraries/scene/lib.rs — scene graph types, double-buffer protocol'). This is now outdated after the triple-buffer migration. The worker's skillFeedback.suggestedChanges recommends updating the skill to document the QEMU sendkey limitation and clock-update pixel diff alternative.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: os-worker SKILL.md line 'Scene graph types and double-buffer protocol (Node fields, Content variants, change list)' and 'system/libraries/scene/lib.rs — scene graph types, double-buffer protocol'. Worker's skillFeedback suggests adding QEMU sendkey limitation documentation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The QEMU sendkey/input-send-event limitation (does not route to virtio-keyboard-device) is documented in CLAUDE.md ('Known') and was known to the worker, but .factory/library/ has no entry for it. This caused the worker to deviate from the feature's verification steps. A library entry would prevent future workers from attempting keyboard automation and immediately adopting the clock-update pixel diff alternative.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues[0]: 'QEMU sendkey/input-send-event does NOT route to virtio-keyboard-device.' Worker deviation: used clock text updates instead of typed text. CLAUDE.md documents it under 'Known' but it's not in .factory/library/.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md architecture.md library entry still describes the scene graph as 'Double-buffered shared memory' in the Rendering Pipeline section, and references 'copy_front_to_back call sites' in the Key Source Files table for scene_state.rs. These references are now stale after triple-buffer migration.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/architecture.md: 'Scene Graph: Double-buffered shared memory' and 'services/core/scene_state.rs — Scene building, copy_front_to_back call sites'. Actual code uses TripleWriter exclusively.

summary: PASS. The integration-verification feature successfully validated the complete pipeline-transport milestone. All 1,791 tests pass (exceeding 1,768 threshold). Build succeeds cleanly. QEMU visual verification confirmed triple-buffer pipeline renders correctly via clock-update pixel diffs (two screenshots 4s apart, 4,635 pixels changed in clock region). Stress test passed for 75 seconds on 4 SMP cores with both fuzz and stress tests passing. Integration test found all 17 boot markers. DoubleWriter/DoubleReader/copy_front_to_back are confirmed absent from all service code. Two non-blocking issues noted: (1) DoubleWriter/DoubleReader dead code (~350 lines) remains in the scene library used only by tests — should be cleaned up per 'kill the old way' claim; (2) keyboard text entry verification was substituted with clock-update pixel diffs due to a known QEMU limitation — reasonable proxy that validates the same rendering pipeline path.
